item.title: Metal you can chew like gum!
item.description: In this video I check out the saying that indium metal is so soft you could chew it like gum or tar in your mouth. So I made a stick of "indium gum" to chew. Before I attempt to chew it, I first show some of the cool features of indium metal like how it "cries" when you bend it. Also I show how it forms an alloy with gallium that has a lower melting point than either of the two metals. It also can make some really neat mirrors since it is able to "wet" glass. Finally I chew my stick of indium. It is surprisingly easy to chew and didn't hurt my teeth at all!

media:content.media:title: Metal you can chew like gum!
media:content.media:description: In this video I check out the saying that indium metal is so soft you could chew it like gum or tar in your mouth. So I made a stick of "indium gum" to chew. Before I attempt to chew it, I first show some of the cool features of indium metal like how it "cries" when you bend it. Also I show how it forms an alloy with gallium that has a lower melting point than either of the two metals. It also can make some really neat mirrors since it is able to "wet" glass. Finally I chew my stick of indium. It is surprisingly easy to chew and didn't hurt my teeth at all!

item.title: Carbonate soda with pop rocks
item.description: In this video I see if it is possible to carbonate soda by adding 22 bags of pop rocks in it. First, in order to get my soda to have no carbonation and be completely flat, I put it in the vacuum chamber. Then I add the pop rocks to pressurize the soda with CO2 and see if it actually adds carbonation to the soda.

media:content.media:title: Carbonate soda with pop rocks
media:content.media:description: In this video I see if it is possible to carbonate soda by adding 22 bags of pop rocks in it. First, in order to get my soda to have no carbonation and be completely flat, I put it in the vacuum chamber. Then I add the pop rocks to pressurize the soda with CO2 and see if it actually adds carbonation to the soda.

item.title: Amazing skittles underwater rainbow waterfall trick!
item.description: In this video I soup up the traditional skittles in water trick that you may have seen done on a plate. I do a 3D version of the color trick. First I show the original skittles in water trick when you add water to skittles candy on a plate. Then I show the more awesome version and talk about what is happening in the process. I talk about some of the science behind why this happens.

media:content.media:title: Amazing skittles underwater rainbow waterfall trick!
media:content.media:description: In this video I soup up the traditional skittles in water trick that you may have seen done on a plate. I do a 3D version of the color trick. First I show the original skittles in water trick when you add water to skittles candy on a plate. Then I show the more awesome version and talk about what is happening in the process. I talk about some of the science behind why this happens.

item.title: How much weight can a fly actually lift?
item.description: In this video I catch a fly and tie a string to it in order to measure how much weight it can lift! First I slow down their metabolism by cooling them down then I tie a string around it and attach duct tape weights to it in order to see how much it can lift! I was surprised how much it could lift!

media:content.media:title: How much weight can a fly actually lift?
media:content.media:description: In this video I catch a fly and tie a string to it in order to measure how much weight it can lift! First I slow down their metabolism by cooling them down then I tie a string around it and attach duct tape weights to it in order to see how much it can lift! I was surprised how much it could lift!

item.title: How are black apples made?
item.description: Disclaimer: Do not try anything you see me do in this video. Ammonia is very toxic and very dangerous to work with.
